entries.definition:
f–r–r to flee, to escape, to take refuge, place of refuge; to be in
a hurry; to shake oneself. Of this root, three forms occur 11 times
in the Qur’an: فَرَّ farra six times; فِرَار firār four times and مَفَرّ
mafarr once.

فَرَّ farra i [v. intrans.] to flee, to fly from (62:8) إِنَّ الْمَوْتَ الَّذِي
تَفِرُّونَ مِنْهُ فَإِنَّهُ مُلَاقِيكُمْ indeed, the death you flee from will [certainly]
come to meet you.

فِرَار firār [v. n./n.] escaping, running away, fleeing; flight
(18:18) لَوِ اطَّلَعْتَ عَلَيْهِمْ لَوَلَّيْتَ مِنْهُمْ فِرَارًا had you seen them, you would
have turned away from them in flight.

مَفَرّ mafarr [v. n./n. of place.] (act of) fleeing; place to which
one flees, refuge (75:10) يَقُولُ الْإِنْسَانُ يَوْمَئِذٍ أَيْنَ الْمَفَرُّ on that Day man
will say, ‘Where is the escape?’ (or, ‘Where to flee?’, or, ‘Where
is the place to escape to?’)
